html:
Exclusive to:

SaladmanderRipe Saladmander

The Saladmander is a neutral Mob exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. It is found on the Lunar Island, specifically the Lunar Baths Biome.

Contents

  • 1 Behavior
  • 2 Fighting
  • 3 Wormwood's Saladmander
  • 4 Trivia
  • 5 Gallery

Behavior

Saladmanders will claim a Hot Spring as their own, and will occasionally fall asleep near it. It will regenerate 5 Health per second while sleeping. If another Saladmander tries to approach a claimed Hot Spring, its owner will run towards them and the two will attempt to intimidate the other into submission by hissing at each other for some time. Shortly after, the "loser" will run away while the "winner" claims the spring. If a Hot Spring has had a Bath Bomb thrown into it, a Saladmander near it will become hot enough to turn ripe shortly.

The player can speed up the ripening process by setting any heat source near the Salamander, for example, a Campfire.

If the player has a Thermal Stone that is at least somewhat hot within their inventory, Saladmanders will hiss at each other until one leaves, and from then they will begin to follow the player until they fall asleep or the thermal stone loses its heat.

Fighting

Saladmanders both Ripe and Unripe have both high health and high damage. However they are slow and have a short range. They can not be stunlocked, but can be kited with 2-3 strikes. Their propensity for sleep can be used to land a few hits. They don't assist each other, so one can attack a Saladmander even if there are other Saladmanders around without having to fear a group attack. Upon death it drops one Leafy Meat in normal form and one Dragon Fruit in Ripe form. Ripe Saladmanders have a flame attack that can set things within 3.5 range around them on fire, and dealing 25 fire damage to them which can't be mitigated by Armor. This flame attack has cooldown of 6 seconds.

Below is the number of hits it takes with each weapon to kill a Saladmander when playing with characters with a default damage modifier. The Weather Pain, Brightshade Staff, and Bee Mine are not included due to their random natures. This table does not take into account the damage bonus gained from characters with a lunar or shadow affinity. It also does not take into account the 40% damage bonus Wendy gets from attacking the same mob as Abigail, Wanda's damage bonus with Shadow weapons (except for the Alarming Clock, which does accurately reflect that bonus), or Wolfgang's damage multiplier when he isn't mighty.

It takes 1 blast with an Ice Staff to freeze Saladmander, and 1 Sleep Dart to put it to sleep.

Wormwood's Saladmander

Wormwood with the Lunar Cultivator III Skill can summon a Saladmander follower at the cost of a Dragon Fruit and 25 HP. Wormwood can have a maximum of 2 Saladmander followers at a time.

Unlike regular Saladmanders:

  • They are slightly smaller.
  • They each have 600 Health if one follower Saladmander is alive and 900 Health if two are alive.
  • They regenerate 5 Health every 2.5 seconds.
  • They deal 40 damage.
  • They don't sleep.
  • They cannot drop Souls.

Wormwood's Saladmanders will engage in combat if their summoner targets an enemy, if an enemy damages their summoner or if they themselves are attacked. After 16 minutes (2 in-game days), or if killed or unsummoned by being attacked, they turn back into a Dragon Fruit. These creatures cannot drop souls.

Trivia

  • Saladmanders were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.
  • As of the Turn of Tides update, they use Merm attack sounds and Grass Gekko hurt and death sounds.
  • Saladmanders can be created again via Steeped Lunar Essence from the Mad Scientist Lab. Making them renewable even if all of them were killed in the lunar island. They can be relocated and fenced in base. Putting a Scaled furnace will caused them to ripen.

Gallery

  • Two Saladmanders hissing at each other
  • Sleeping Saladmander
  • Saladmanders as seen in a drawing from Art Stream # 90.

SaladmanderRipe Saladmander
“It's cute, but it's not ripe yet.”
–Wilson, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“They're pretty cute.”
–Willow, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“Is little planty scrambler!”
–Wolfgang, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“It prefers to be alone.”
–Wendy, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“HA HA. THEY HATE EACH OTHER”
–WX-78, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“Plant and animal have merged into one in this specimen.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“Just a sweet little critter.”
–Woodie, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“They aren't very sociable.”
–Maxwell, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“Meat impöstör!”
–Wigfrid, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“It smells like yummy fruit.”
–Webber, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“Our little friend is not quite ripe.”
–Warly, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“Growing belly stuff”
–Wormwood, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“You play nice with the other lizards now, hey?”
–Winona, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“Little lizard, sharp and pointy!”
–Wortox, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“It not easy being green.”
–Wurt, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“It's a little lizard thing!”
–Walter, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“What a funny little creature.”
–Wanda, when examining an unripe Saladmander.
“I think it's ripe now.”
–Wilson, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“Mmm... Roast dragonfruit.”
–Willow, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“Planty scrambler smells delicious today.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“You simply look too delicious.”
–Wendy, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“I WANT TO EAT IT”
–WX-78, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“It does smell quite delicious.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“Were you always that colourful?”
–Woodie, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“I bet it's delicious.”
–Maxwell, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“I have nö need for your fake meat.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“It looks kinda yummy.”
–Webber, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“You'd be perfect in a meal!”
–Warly, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“Feeling warm...”
–Wormwood, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“Lookit you, all orange!”
–Winona, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“That color looks great on you, hyuyu!”
–Wortox, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“Smell like... fruit?”
–Wurt, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“I could've sworn it was green a second ago.”
–Walter, when examining a ripe Saladmander.
“It's a shame they're so delicious.”
–Wanda, when examining a ripe Saladmander.

Wormwood with the Lunar Cultivator III Skill can summon a Saladmander follower at the cost of a Dragon Fruit and 25 HP. Wormwood can have a maximum of 2 Saladmander followers at a time.
Unlike regular Saladmanders:
They are slightly smaller.
They each have 600 Health if one follower Saladmander is alive and 900 Health if two are alive.
They regenerate 5 Health every 2.5 seconds.
They deal 40 damage.
They don't sleep.
They cannot drop Souls.
Wormwood's Saladmanders will engage in combat if their summoner targets an enemy, if an enemy damages their summoner or if they themselves are attacked. After 16 minutes (2 in-game days), or if killed or unsummoned by being attacked, they turn back into a Dragon Fruit.
These creatures cannot drop souls.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Saladmanders were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.
As of the Turn of Tides update, they use Merm attack sounds and Grass Gekko hurt and death sounds.
Saladmanders can be created again via Steeped Lunar Essence from the Mad Scientist Lab. Making them renewable even if all of them were killed in the lunar island. They can be relocated and fenced in base. Putting a Scaled furnace will caused them to ripen.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Saladmander là một Mob trung lập độc quyền trong Don't Starve Together, được giới thiệu trong Return of Them. Nó được tìm thấy trên Đảo Mặt Trăng, đặc biệt là Quần xã Phòng Tắm Mặt Trăng.
title: Saladmander
wikitext:
{{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}} {{Object Infobox |image = Saladmander.png|Normal Saladmander Ripe.png|Ripe |name = Saladmander |damage = {{Damagereceived|30| (normal)}}{{Damagereceived|50| (ripe)}} |attackPeriod = 2 |attackRange = 2 |walkSpeed = 0.5 |runSpeed = 2 |specialAbility = Flame burst attack that sets players and other flammable items around it on fire (ripe). |drops = {{Pic24|Leafy Meat}} (normal)
{{Pic24|Dragon Fruit}} (ripe) |spawnCode = "fruitdragon" |health = 900 |lunarAligned = 1 }} Saladmander= {{Quotes |wilson = It's cute, but it's not ripe yet. |willow = They're pretty cute. |wolfgang = Is little planty scrambler! |wendy = It prefers to be alone. |wx78 = HA HA. THEY HATE EACH OTHER |wickerbottom = Plant and animal have merged into one in this specimen. |woodie = Just a sweet little critter. |maxwell = They aren't very sociable. |wigfrid = Meat impöstör! |webber = It smells like yummy fruit. |winona = You play nice with the other lizards now, hey? |warly = Our little friend is not quite ripe. |wortox = Little lizard, sharp and pointy! |wormwood = Growing belly stuff |wurt = It not easy being green. |walter = It's a little lizard thing! |wanda = What a funny little creature. |choose = woodie |source = when examining an unripe Saladmander.}} |-| Ripe Saladmander= {{Quotes |wilson = I think it's ripe now. |willow = Mmm... Roast dragonfruit. |wolfgang = Planty scrambler smells delicious today. |wendy = You simply look too delicious. |wx78 = I WANT TO EAT IT |wickerbottom = It does smell quite delicious. |woodie = Were you always that colourful? |maxwell = I bet it's delicious. |wigfrid = I have nö need for your fake meat. |webber = It looks kinda yummy. |winona = Lookit you, all orange! |warly = You'd be perfect in a meal! |wortox = That color looks great on you, hyuyu! |wormwood = Feeling warm... |wurt = Smell like... fruit? |walter = I could've sworn it was green a second ago. |wanda = It's a shame they're so delicious. |choose = wilson |source = when examining a ripe Saladmander.}} The '''Saladmander''' is a neutral [[Mobs/DST|Mob]] exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. It is found on the [[Lunar Island]], specifically the Lunar Baths Biome. === {{Pic32|Brain}} Behavior === Saladmanders will claim a [[Hot Spring]] as their own, and will occasionally fall asleep near it. It will regenerate 5 Health per second while sleeping. If another Saladmander tries to approach a claimed Hot Spring, its owner will run towards them and the two will attempt to intimidate the other into submission by hissing at each other for some time. Shortly after, the "loser" will run away while the "winner" claims the spring. If a Hot Spring has had a Bath Bomb thrown into it, a Saladmander near it will become hot enough to turn ripe shortly. The player can speed up the ripening process by setting any heat source near the Salamander, for example, a [[Campfire]]. If the player has a [[Thermal Stone]] that is at least somewhat hot within their inventory, Saladmanders will hiss at each other until one leaves, and from then they will begin to follow the player until they fall asleep or the thermal stone loses its heat. === {{Pic|32|Spear}} Fighting === Saladmanders both Ripe and Unripe have both high health and high damage. However they are slow and have a short range. They can not be stunlocked, but can be kited with 2-3 strikes. Their propensity for sleep can be used to land a few hits. They don't assist each other, so one can attack a Saladmander even if there are other Saladmanders around without having to fear a group attack. Upon death it drops one [[Leafy Meat]] in normal form and one [[Dragon Fruit]] in Ripe form. Ripe Saladmanders have a flame attack that can set things within 3.5 range around them on fire, and dealing 25 fire damage to them which can't be mitigated by [[Armor]]. This flame attack has cooldown of 6 seconds. {{Weapon Hits DST|health=900|entityalignment=lunar|text={{Pic|32|Saladmander Ripe}}|icestaff=1|sleepdart=1}} {{clear}} == {{picDark|48|Lunar Cultivator III}} Wormwood's Saladmander == {{Object Infobox |image = Saladmander.png |icon = Saladmander Item.png |name = Saladmander |damage = 40 |attackPeriod = 2 |attackRange = 2 |walkSpeed = 0.5 |runSpeed = 2 |drops = {{Pic24|Dragon Fruit}} |health = 600 or 900 |lunarAligned=1 |ingredient1=Dragon Fruit |multiplier1=1 |ingredient2=Health Decrease |multiplier2=25 ({{Pic|28|Wormwood Portrait|Wormwood}} only) |filter={{Pic32|Wormwood Filter}} |skill = Lunar Cultivator III |skillOwner = Wormwood |lunarAligned = 1 }} Wormwood with the [[Lunar Cultivator III]] Skill can summon a Saladmander follower at the cost of a Dragon Fruit and 25 HP. Wormwood can have a maximum of 2 Saladmander followers at a time. Unlike regular Saladmanders: * They are slightly smaller. * They each have 600 Health if one follower Saladmander is alive and 900 Health if two are alive. * They regenerate 5 Health every 2.5 seconds. * They deal 40 damage. * They don't sleep. * They cannot drop [[Souls]]. Wormwood's Saladmanders will engage in combat if their summoner targets an enemy, if an enemy damages their summoner or if they themselves are attacked. After 16 minutes (2 in-game days), or if killed or unsummoned by being attacked, they turn back into a Dragon Fruit. These creatures cannot drop souls. {{clear}} == {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * Saladmanders were introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Turn of Tides|Turn of Tides]]'' update. * As of the ''Turn of Tides'' update, they use [[Merm]] attack sounds and [[Grass Gekko]] hurt and death sounds. *Saladmanders can be created again via [[Lunar Experiment|Steeped Lunar Essence]] from the [[Mad Scientist Lab]]. Making them renewable even if all of them were killed in the lunar island. They can be relocated and fenced in base. Putting a [[Scaled Furnace|Scaled furnace]] will caused them to ripen. == {{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Saladmander_Fight.png|Two Saladmanders hissing at each other Saladmander Sleeping.png|Sleeping Saladmander Art Stream 90 Saladmander.png|Saladmanders as seen in a drawing from Art Stream # 90. {{clear}} Craftable Items= {{Craftable Items and Structures on DST}} |-| Mobs= {{Mobs}} [[vi:Saladmander]] [[Category:Return of Them]] [[Category:Neutral Creatures]] [[Category:Fire Starter]]
